items.title: Germany's WWII munitions a toxic legacy on Baltic Sea floor
items.summary: KIEL, (Germany): Below the waves off Germany's northern tourist beaches, a toxic time bomb lurks on the Baltic Sea floor – enormous quantities of World War II munitions that are slowly rusting away. To better map the dangers, a research vessel set sail this month from the port city of Kiel, whose bay is among the most polluted with unexploded ordnance. Along the German coast, about 1.6 million tonnes of munitions litter the seafloor, especially near the ports of Kiel and Luebeck, making it one of the world's most contaminated areas.

items.title: Malaysia steps up food security measures as West Asia conflict disrupts supply chains, says minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 29 — The government will continue to strengthen the country’s food system to ensure sufficient supply and access to the people during the conflict in West Asia. Minister of Agriculture and Food Security Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said it is a responsibility that we are currently carrying out. He said that in today’s world, the geopolitical conflict in West Asia is not just an external issue, as it also affects the global food supply chain.

items.title: MOF: Govt maintains fuel subsidies to cushion impact of rising global oil prices
items.summary: In a Facebook post today, the Ministry of Finance (MOF) said the government is continuing its efforts through the Subsidised Petrol Control System (SKPS) and the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS), which provide targeted fuel subsidies for the land and water public transport sectors, as well as for the transportation of selected goods. “These measures help keep transportation costs under control and ensure that commodity prices remain stable,” the ministry said. Meanwhile, under SKDS, the subsidised diesel price for the land transport sector remains at RM1.88 per litre, while diesel for goods transportation continues to be capped at RM2.15 per litre.

items.title: Concerns grow over aggressive 'freelance photographers' at KLCC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Concerns are mounting over the behaviour of unlicensed photographers operating around the Petronas Twin Towers, with visitors reporting aggressive tactics and growing unease at one of the city's busiest tourist spots. Groups of men offering paid photography services — often approaching both local and foreign visitors — have been active in the area, particularly in the evenings, prompting questions over enforcement and public safety. Some visitors said the photographers compete for customers, at times creating a tense atmosphere.

items.title: Anwar: Egypt guarantees smooth passage for Malaysian aid into Gaza
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 29 — Egypt has given a clear guarantee and commitment to facilitate the distribution of 374 tonnes of humanitarian aid from Malaysia to the Palestinian population in Gaza through the Rafah border crossing starting this April, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. “Alhamdulillah, President El-Sisi has given a clear guarantee and commitment to ensure that this access is facilitated, thus enabling immediate aid to be distributed to more than 100,000 Gazans who are in dire need,” he said in a Facebook post. “Please pray that all matters are facilitated and that this aid reaches our brothers in Gaza safely, God willing,” he said.

items.title: Five Apple anecdotes as iPhone maker marks 50 years
items.summary: PARIS: Phone maker Apple celebrates its 50th anniversary on Apr 1, having marked pop culture and the tech industry like few other firms since its beginnings in 1976. Apple logo Designer Rob Janoff said that Apple co-founder Steve Jobs gave him one terse instruction when he commissioned a new logo in January 1977: "don't make it cute." Apple's third man Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak have gone down in history as Apple's co-founders.

items.title: Police arrest five men over road rage incident in Kota Kinabalu
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Five men were arrested to assist in investigations into an attack on a vehicle here. Acting district police chief, Superintendent Syed Lot Syed Ab Rahman, said police received a report on the matter at 2.35pm the same day. He said initial investigations found that the victim, a 30-year-old teacher, had been involved in a verbal provocation with the five men.

items.title: Low OSH compliance among industries a serious concern
items.summary: LETTERS: The issue of low Occupational Safety and Health (OSH) compliance among industries, particularly small and medium enterprises (SMEs), has been a matter of serious concern. Workplace safety must never be treated as optional or secondary. Low OSH compliance exposes workers to preventable accidents, injuries, occupational diseases and even fatalities.

items.title: Universiti Malaya secures world-first dental accreditation from Royal College of Surgeons England
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 29 — Universiti Malaya (UM) has become the first institution globally to be accredited as a Dental Education Centre by the Faculty of Dental Surgery of the Royal College of Surgeons of England. In a statement today, UM said the award was presented by Faculty of Dental Surgery dean Dr Charlotte Eckhardt to Deputy Vice-Chancellor (Student Affairs) Professor Dr Zamri Radzi, who represented the vice-chancellor, together with Faculty of Dentistry dean Professor Dr Firdaus Hariri. UM said the recognition affirms that its dental speciality doctoral programmes meet rigorous academic and clinical standards set by the Faculty of Dental Surgery.

items.title: ‘No such arrangement’: Petronas denies report of alleged fuel supply deal in Philippines
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 29 — Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) has denied any involvement in a reported fuel supply deal linked to a local government unit in the Philippines. It added that its immediate priority remains ensuring a reliable and continuous fuel supply for Malaysia, supported by established supply networks and close coordination with the government, it said in a statement today. — Bernama

items.title: Kuantan pensioner loses RM337,000 in Facebook investment scam
items.summary: Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the woman contacted the suspect via WhatsApp and was instructed to carry out multiple financial transactions. He said she made 38 transfers into 16 different bank accounts using her savings and pawned jewellery between January 30 and March 9. “All investment-related matters, including profits, were handled solely via WhatsApp without any legitimate platform,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Armizan: Papar water disruptions caused by shortage, saltwater intrusion
items.summary: PAPAR: Recurring water supply disruptions here are caused by a shortage of treated water and saltwater intrusion affecting key treatment plants, said Papar member of parliament Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali. He said the district requires about 93 million litres (MLD) of treated water daily, but existing treatment plants can only produce 63 MLD. "The main issue is the shortage of treated water supply.

items.title: DPM Fadillah: Malaysia taps AI, flood forensics to boost water security under AIR2040
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 29 — The Energy Transition and Water Transformation Ministry (Petra), through the National Water Research Institute of Malaysia (Nahrim), is intensifying efforts to strengthen national water security by leveraging science, technology and innovation in line with the 2040 Water Sector Transformation Agenda (AIR2040) under the 13th Malaysia Plan. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, who is also the Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister, said a research-driven and evidence-based approach is central to ensuring sustainable and resilient water resource management, particularly in addressing climate change and rising water demand. Flood management is also being enhanced through surplus water storage technologies such as riverbed reservoirs, nature-based solutions and multi-functional reservoirs.

items.title: Fuel price drops, diesel price rises further in Cambodia
items.summary: PHNOM PENH, (Cambodia): Retail fuel price in Cambodia has declined, as diesel price increased further due to the impact of the West Asia conflict, the Ministry of Commerce announced around midnight on Saturday, reported Xinhua. A litre of regular fuel will cost 5,000 riels (US$1.25) from Sunday until the next notice, down 8.2 per cent from 5,450 riels in the past three days, said the ministry, adding that diesel will go for 7,200 riels (US$1.80) per litre, up 1.4 per cent from 7,100 riels. Also on Sunday, the price of Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG) climbed to 3,400 riels (US$0.85) per litre, up 6.2 per cent from 3,200 riels.

items.title: Nearly 60 hectares of Rompin plantation fire extinguished, authorities say
items.summary: KUANTAN: Nearly 60 hectares of a 300-hectare fire at an oil palm plantation in Rompin had been extinguished as of 5pm yesterday, authorities said. The Pahang Fire and Rescue Department said 85 water-bombing runs had been carried out, using 68,000 litres of water to contain the blaze. Its assistant director of operations and rescue, Mohd Salahuddin Isa, said the firefighting area had been divided into four sectors.

items.title: Rompin’s 300-hectare fire still burning after 85 water-bombing runs, say firefighters
items.summary: Pahang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant director of operations and rescue, Mohd Salahuddin Isa, said 85 water-bombing runs had been carried out, using 68,000 litres of water to contain the blaze. Meanwhile, according to the Air Pollutant Index Management System, air quality in Rompin was unhealthy, with an Air Pollutant Index (API) reading of 152 as of 10am. Pahang Department of Environment director Zakaria Ahmad said checks would be carried out to determine whether the fire or other factors caused the unhealthy API reading.

items.title: Fighting fit: India flexes military strength
items.summary: The low-level, supersonic pass of India's latest multirole combat aircraft signalled the official commencement of Exercise Vayushakti 2026, a high-intensity demonstration of India's air combat capability and joint operations. Over the course of a two-week familiarisation trip, the New Straits Times was granted exclusive access to India's navy, army and air force, getting a firsthand look at the country's operational readiness, technological integration and growing self-reliance in defence capabilities. There, India displayed its indigenous naval assets, including the aircraft carrier INS Vikrant, submarines and frontline combatants.

items.title: Drug-related cases rise to 242,075 with over 300,000 arrests nationwide
items.summary: Narcotics Crime Investigation Department director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said the figures showed a steady upward trend in drug-related offences each year. He said the number of cases rose from 73,340 in 2023 to 81,090 in 2024, before reaching 87,645 last year. Hussein added that investigations into trafficking cases were conducted under Section 39B, Section 39A(2), Section 39A(1) and Section 6B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, while possession cases were investigated under Section 12(3) and Section 6 of the same Act, as well as Section 9 and Section 30(3) of the Poisons Act 1952.

items.title: No tsunami risk to Malaysia after strong quake in Indonesia’s Banda Sea, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 29 — There is no tsunami threat to Malaysia following a strong earthquake that struck Banda Sea, Indonesia, early this morning. Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) director-general Dr Mohd Hisham Mohd Anip said the strong 6.0 magnitude earthquake occurred at 2.38am. He said the epicentre of the earthquake was located at coordinates 7.9 degrees south and 127.8 degrees east at a depth of 173 kilometres (km).

items.title: Penang RTD issues 2,724 summonses during 13-day Hari Raya operation
items.summary: Its director, Zulkifly Ismail, said 19,900 vehicles were inspected, with 1,492 found to have violated traffic regulations. A total of 13 vehicles were also seized for various offences," he said. Private vehicles recorded the highest number of cases at 1,860, followed by motorcycles (500), goods vehicles exceeding 7,500kg (253), goods vehicles below 7,500kg (83), and public service vehicles, including buses (19).

items.title: Iran attack on Aluminium Bahrain plant wounds two: state media
items.summary: Manama, March 29, 2026 (AFP) - Two of Aluminium Bahrain's employees were wounded in an Iranian attack on its facilities over the weekend, Bahrain state media reported on Sunday. Since the Middle East war erupted at the end of February, Bahrain and other Gulf countries have been regularly targeted by Iranian missile and drone strikes in retaliation for the US-Israeli campaign, now in its second month. "The safety and security of (Aluminium Bahrain's) people remain its top priority and the Company confirms that two of Alba's employees sustained minor injuries," the company said in a statement carried by the official Bahrain News Agency.

items.title: ICT Zone earnings outlook lifted by steady order flow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: ICT Zone Asia Bhd's latest purchase order for information and communications technology (ICT) hardware trading is expected to contribute about RM1.5 million to its net profit. TA Securities Holdings Bhd said the contract highlights the group's strong footing this year and reinforces its near-term revenue visibility. "Assuming a net margin of six per cent, in line with our trading segment assumptions, the order is estimated to contribute approximately RM1.5 million to net profit upon fulfilment, providing incremental support to near-term earnings.

items.title: Sabah to crack down on 'Ali Baba' practices in tourism sector
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The Sabah government will consider measures to curb "Ali Baba" practices among tour operators, as part of efforts to protect the state's tourism industry. State Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said the move is aimed at preventing the misuse of local names by third parties. Jafry said about 40 industry players have been identified as holding valid licences, with the list published on the Tourism, Arts and Culture Malaysia Ministry (Motac) website for public reference.

items.title: Deputy minister eyes direct Miri-China charter flights in tourism push
items.summary: MIRI, March 29 — The Tourism, Arts and Culture Ministry recently held an engagement session with Chinese tour operators involved in developing charter flight packages. Deputy Minister Chiew Choon Man, who delivered a presentation during a segment of the programme, said he encouraged the operators to explore new direct flight routes between Miri and China. My goal is to put Miri firmly on the global tourism map, especially as one of Sarawak’s largest cities.

items.title: Retiree loses RM337k to bogus investment scheme
items.summary: The victim fell for a scheme that had no legitimate platform and communicated solely through WhatsApp. Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the complainant came across the "Malaysia Industrial Development Finance Bhd (MIDF)" scheme on Facebook on Jan 29. "The victim communicated with an individual through WhatsApp.

items.title: Nearly 1,500 found flouting traffic rules in Penang during 13-day Aidilfitri ops
items.summary: Its director, Zulkifly Ismail, said 19,900 vehicles were inspected during the period, with 1,492 found violating traffic rules. A total of 13 vehicles were also seized for various offences. “Private vehicles recorded the highest number of cases at 1,860, followed by motorcycles (500), goods vehicles exceeding 7,500 kg (253), goods vehicles below 7,500 kg (83), and public service vehicles, including buses (19),” he said.

items.title: Parking dispute turns violent in Kota Kinabalu, teacher injured in alleged mob attack
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 29 — A minor parking mishap escalated into a violent mob attack, leaving a teacher injured and traumatised in what he described as a “lawless” ordeal. The incident occurred yesterday afternoon near a shipping company warehouse close to the Kota Kinabalu port, where the victim had parked amid heavy congestion during the state government Aidilfitri open house at the Sabah International Convention Centre (SICC). In a panic, he said attempted to leave but accidentally struck another vehicle while reversing.

items.title: Kelantan warns couples to legalise divorce to prevent future conflicts
items.summary: Kelantan Islamic Development, Da'wah, Information and Public Relations Committee chairman Mohd Asri Mat Daud said every divorce, including those involving "taklik" (conditional) clauses, must be confirmed at an Islamic religious office or Syariah Court to prevent misunderstandings and potential slander. "From a religious perspective, the new marriage is valid if it meets the pillars and conditions, but problems arise when the previous divorce is not legally confirmed," he told Bernama. In Kelantan, Mohd Asri said the Kelantan Islamic Religious Affairs Department (JAHEAIK) is investigating such cases by summoning imams, kadis and marriage officials involved in the marriage contract to obtain further information.

items.title: Sabah hosts country's first international stand-up paddle marathon
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah has hosted the country's first International Stand-up Paddle (SUP) marathon off Tunku Abdul Rahman Park here. State Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said the event could serve as a platform to promote Sabah to international tourists. Jafry said that for the longest distance category, participants passed Pulau Gaya, Pulau Sapi, Pulau Manukan, Pulau Suluk and Pulau Mamutik before returning to the starting point.

items.title: Pope denounces widening gap between the rich and poor on Monaco visit
items.summary: MONACO: Pope Leo XIV on Saturday denounced the widening gap between the haves and have-nots as he visited Monaco, a millionaires' playground that was the surprise pick for the first western European trip of his papacy. Arriving by helicopter from Rome as the first pope to visit the principality in nearly half a millennium, Leo was greeted by Monaco's ruler Prince Albert II and his wife, Princess Charlene, at Monte Carlo's heliport. Just after his arrival at the tiny principality on the French Riviera, the pope condemned what he termed the widening "chasms between the poor and the rich."

items.title: Motorcyclist, 17, dies in Besut crash after attempted overtake at junction
items.summary: JERTIH, March 29 — A boy, 17, was killed when the motorcycle he was riding hit another motorcycle in Kampung Telaga Nibong, Hulu Besut near here yesterday. Besut district police chief Supt Mohd Rozaime Ab Rahim said Muhammad Haziq Azman, a chicken sales assistant, died at the scene while the other motorcyclist suffered minor injuries during the 11.30am crash. “But at the same time, the victim, on a Honda Wave 125 came from behind and tried to overtake the Honda EX5,” he said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: Food prices may rise up to 50pct following fuel hike, says association [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Food prices are expected to rise by up to 50 per cent following the recent increase in fuel costs, the Federation of Malaysian Hawkers and Traders Associations said. "Even before the fuel price increase, prices had already risen by around 20 to 30 per cent. Meanwhile, nasi lemak seller Mohammad Syah Bedoh Lahata, 30, said he will have to raise his selling prices if the cost of raw materials increased sharply.

items.title: Cameron Highlands farmers on alert as heatwave threatens vegetable yields
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Vegetable farmers in Cameron Highlands are on alert following the prolonged hot and dry spell in the northern states, with past weather patterns suggesting similar conditions could reach the highlands by April or May. Cameron Highlands Chinese Farmers Association deputy president Cheng Nan Hong said historical trends show that when temperatures in northern states exceed 30°C for a prolonged period, the highlands typically average at 23°C, with greenhouse temperatures reaching 28°C. He said such temperatures were not optimal for temperate vegetable farming and could significantly reduce yields.

items.title: 'Yields could fall by up to 80pct': Farmers raise concerns over Mada's dry seeding plan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Padi farmers have raised concerns over the Muda Agricultural Development Authority's (Mada) push for dry direct seeding for the upcoming planting season, warning it could cut yields by up to 80 per cent and leave farmers to bear all losses. The initiative was introduced following a prolonged hot and dry spell that has depleted water resources in the region, forcing Mada to prioritise domestic water supply over padi irrigation. Malaysian Padi Farmers Brotherhood Organisation (Pesawah) chairman Abdul Rashid Yob said dry direct seeding carried high risks, with about only two in 10 farmers likely to achieve a successful harvest due to pests, weeds and poor nutrient uptake.

items.title: Ringgit to hold near RM4 next week amid hawkish Fed, oil shock
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit is expected to trade around the 4.00 level against the US dollar next week, as expectations of a prolonged Middle East conflict weigh on the currency. Kenanga Investment Bank Bhd (Kenanga IB) said investors are likely to remain overweight on the greenback as a hedge against renewed escalation once the US 10-day pause on strikes against Iran's energy facilities ends. "The ringgit's initial outperformance faded in a late-cycle correction, as the benchmark Brent crude oil holding near US$100 per barrel has heightened US inflation concerns.

items.title: School bus operators may revise fares amid diesel price hike, says association
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Rising diesel prices are expected to affect the transport sector, with a possible increase in school bus fares if cost pressures persisted. Its president Amali Munif Rahmat said the real impact would begin to be felt, including higher maintenance costs, spare parts and engine oil, if diesel prices remained high over a certain period. "When transport costs increase, it will affect the entire chain, including the prices of goods and other services, even if the impact is not immediate.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia to remain cautious next week as West Asia conflict dents sentiment
items.summary: On the index board, the FBM Top 100 Index declined 51.29 points to 12,337.50, the FBM Emas Index lost 73.54 points to 12,465.26, and the FBM Mid 70 Index slipped 44.19 points to 17,098.79. The weekly turnover expanded to 12.24 billion units worth RM13.69 billion against 11.88 billion units worth RM14.56 billion a week earlier. The Main Market volume improved to 7.64 billion units worth RM12.81 billion against 7.41 billion units worth RM13.74 billion previously.

items.title: Sarawak air quality worsens as 17 areas record moderate API levels
items.summary: “The increase in API levels is likely due to localised open burning and transboundary haze. If dry weather conditions persist and biomass burning activities increase, Sarawak risks experiencing haze,” the statement read. “In this regard, NREB has taken various proactive measures, including activating its haze operations room, conducting field patrols, temporarily suspending the issuance of controlled burning permits and issuing notices prohibiting open burning to all project developers.

items.title: Pentagon preparing for weeks of ground operations in Iran
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The Pentagon is preparing for weeks of ground operations in Iran, the Washington Post reported on Saturday, citing US officials. The plans could involve raids by Special Operations and conventional infantry troops, the Post reported. Whether President Donald Trump would approve any of those plans remains uncertain, according to the Post.

items.title: Budi95 adjustment seen as pragmatic amid rising oil prices
items.summary: Sabah and Labuan Chinese Chamber of Commerce deputy secretary Weal Cheoh said that although the quota had been temporarily reduced from 300 litres to 200 litres per month, the government's decision to maintain subsidies amid rising global oil prices reflected its continued commitment to safeguarding public welfare. "In the Malaysian context, the majority of private vehicles have engine capacities below 2,000cc and use around 200 litres per month, which is sufficient for normal usage," he said. "Although the quota was temporarily reduced from 300 litres to 200 litres, it remained relevant and does not have a significant impact on most users," he told Bernama.

items.title: Teen drowns while swimming with friends in Ranau river
items.summary: RANAU: A 15-year-old boy drowned while swimming with three friends in Sungai Liwagu in Kampung Paginatan here yesterday. Ranau district police chief Superintendent Mursalin Mahmud said police received an emergency call at 5.06pm regarding the incident. "Preliminary checks found no other injuries on the victim's body.

items.title: Police probe alleged cable theft in Ipoh after video goes viral on TikTok
items.summary: IPOH, March 29 — Police are seeking public assistance over a suspected cable theft at a residential area in Jalan Pakatan Jaya, Taman Pakatan Jaya here, which has gone viral on social media. Ipoh police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said police came across the video on TikTok, uploaded by the account Utusan Melayu Plus (@utusanmelayuplusofficial) at about 11am yesterday. “Police have opened an investigation paper and the case is being investigated under Section 379 of the Penal Code,” he said in a statement last night.

items.title: MetMalaysia: No tsunami threat to Malaysia after Banda Sea quake
items.summary: "The incident occurred 467km south of Ambon, Indonesia, at 2.38am today," he said in a statement. In a separate statement, MetMalaysia issued a warning of thunderstorms and heavy rain expected in Perak, Selangor and Sarawak this morning. It said the weather conditions would affect Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Klang and Kuala Langat in Selangor until 9am.

items.title: #NSTviral: Children warm tourists' hearts with duit raya gesture
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A viral video showing a group of kampung children presenting duit raya to a foreign tourist has captured the hearts of netizens. Upon opening the packet, the tourist discovered RM2 inside. One (roti) canai biasa?

items.title: Heatwave forces 206 schools in Kedah to switch to home learning
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, March 29 — Schools in Kota Setar and Pendang will shift to Home-Based Teaching and Learning (PdPR) for three days, from today until Tuesday, due to the ongoing heatwave. The Kedah State Education Department (JPN) said the decision follows reports from the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), which recorded Level 2 heatwave temperatures in both districts from March 26 to 28. The Kedah JPN will continue to monitor weather conditions and provide updates on school operations as needed.

items.title: MPPs probed over extortion: What does an MPP actually do?
items.summary: The two men, members of the Residents Representative Council (MPP), have been asked to resign. What is an MPP? MPP members are appointed by the Federal Territories Minister.

items.title: Police nab Chinese national for allegedly filming man in Bukit Jalil toilet mall
items.summary: Cheras district police chief ACP Mohd Rosdi Daud said the suspect, in his 20s, was nabbed at 1.28 pm following a report lodged by the victim. “Following the arrest, police seized the suspect’s mobile phone, and a preliminary check found several lewd videos in the gallery,” he said in a statement yesterday. Mohd Rosdi added that initial investigations revealed the suspect admitted to the act, while further checks showed he did not possess valid travel documents.

items.title: Why farmers and traders in Malaysia are bracing for a veg price crunch this April: What’s behind the Hormuz Strait connection
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — Vegetables staple to the Malaysian diet could soon take a bigger bite out of household budgets, with prices expected to rise as early as April as the war in West Asia disrupts global supply chains. At Pasar Dato Keramat, vegetable seller Romlah Nasir said she is still clearing stock delivered last Thursday. “We also cannot raise the prices of items like vegetables and coconut milk now because the government has enforced price controls until March 28 for Aidilfitri,” she said.

items.title: Khairy says won't seek position if returning to Umno
items.summary: PONTIAN, March 28 — Former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin said he intends to return to Umno as an ordinary member without seeking any position as he is still in the process of rejoining the party. “I have submitted the application letter and leave it to Umno to make a decision,” he told reporters after attending an Aidilfitri open house hosted by Umno executive secretary and Pontian Umno deputy chief Datuk Mohd Sumali Reduan in Benut here. Earlier today, Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin was reported as saying that applications from several former leaders, including Khairy, would be brought to the Supreme Council meeting.

items.title: Cops hunt individuals seen firing fireworks from car sunroof in Shah Alam
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 28 — Police are tracking several individuals suspected of setting off fireworks in a dangerous manner from the sunroof of a moving car at Persiaran Ficus, Section U15, here recently. Shah Alam police chief ACP Ramsay Embol said a report on the incident was lodged at 12.48pm today, after the video of the act went viral. He confirmed that the incident occurred in the Elmina Rainforest area, Persiaran Ficus, Section U15.

items.title: More Kedah districts record Level 2 heatwave as temperatures stay high
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — Four districts in Kedah — Baling, Pendang, Pokok Sena and Kota Setar — have recorded Level 2 heatwaves as of 4pm. According to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), Level 1 heatwaves are also recorded in Bandar Baharu, Kulim, Kuala Muda, Sik, Padang Terap, Kubang Pasu and Pulau Langkawi in Kedah, along with Perlis; Utara district in Penang; Perak (Kuala Kangsar, Kinta and Hulu Perak) and Lubok Antu in Sarawak. Level 2 heatwaves are designated for areas with a maximum daily temperature between 37 and 40 degrees Celsius for at least three consecutive days, while Level 1 heatwaves are for temperatures between 35 and 37 degrees for at least three consecutive days.

items.title: Hari Raya return travel picks up on key highways nationwide in the evening
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — Traffic is slow along several major highways in the country as of this evening following the increase in vehicles, the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) said. Slow traffic has been detected along the north-bound North-South Expressway (PLUS) E1 from Jawi to Bandar Cassia, Permatang Pauh to Bertam, Sungai Petani (South) to Gurun, Alor Setar (North) to Hutan Kampung Toll Plaza and Jitra (South) to Jitra Toll Plaza, while for the south-bound route, slow traffic has been reported from Kepala Batas to Hutan Kampung Toll Plaza, Pendang to Gurun, Sungai Petani (North) to Sungai Petani (South), Sungai Dua to Perai, Juru to Bukit Tambun, Sungai Perak to Menora Tunnel, Tapah to Slim River and Sungai Buloh Hospital to Sungai Buloh. “Along the north-bound PLUS E2, slow traffic has been reported from Pasir Gudang to Kempas, Senai to Sedenak, Yong Peng to Pagoh, Pedas Linggi to Seremban, Putra Mahkota to Kajang and towards the city centre after Sungai Besi Toll Plaza, while for the south-bound route, traffic is slow from Sungai Besi Toll Plaza to Bangi, Nilai to Seremban R&R, Port Dickson to Hentian Senawang, Tangkak to Bukit Gambir, Simpang Renggam to Kulai and Kempas to Pasir Gudang,” the LLM said when contacted today, adding that 34 Smart Lanes along PLUS have been activated throughout the day.

items.title: Health Ministry issues guidelines to reduce heatwave risks, urges less outdoor exposure
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 28 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has advised the public to limit time spent outdoors and reduce physical activity as key measures to mitigate health risks during the current heatwave. In a statement today, the MOH outlined six primary guidelines, which include ensuring the body remains hydrated by drinking plenty of water regardless of the level of physical activity. To maintain body cooling, the MOH suggested using fans or air conditioning during periods of high temperature.

items.title: Govt can sustain RON95 subsidy for now, may adjust if costs rise, says finance minister’s aide
items.summary: IPOH, March 28 — The government is still able to sustain the RON95 petrol subsidy for now but is prepared to adjust its policy if costs continue to rise due to the crisis in West Asia. Political secretary to the Finance Minister, Muhammad Kamil Abdul Munim, said being prepared is crucial to ensure economic stability without neglecting public welfare, despite the risk of rising global oil prices. On Petronas’ profits following higher global oil prices, he said Malaysia still needs to import oil to meet domestic demand.

items.title: Padi subsidies a crucial buffer against rising costs, say farmers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — Padi farmers have welcomed the government’s decision to maintain input subsidies and cash assistance, describing them as crucial amid rising global agricultural costs following tensions in West Asia. He said the assistance helps ease initial capital burdens and enables farmers to continue cultivation despite rising input prices. This greatly assists farmers,” he told Bernama.

items.title: Eight-year-old girl dies in Taiping: Police suspect abuse and arrest four family members
items.summary: IPOH, March 28 — An eight-year-old girl, suspected of being a victim of abuse, died in Taiping on Thursday. The child had several injury marks on her body. Taiping district police chief ACP Abdul Malek Ismail said his team received a report of the victim's death from a medical officer at Taiping Hospital.

items.title: Under remand till March 30: Three men, suspected of involvement in attack on police in Penang apartment
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 28 — The remand of three of eight men detained to assist investigations into an alleged attack on police in an apartment area in Jalan Sungai here on Wednesday night has been extended by three days. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the three local men in their 30s are remanded until March 30, while the other five, previously remanded for three days, were released on police bail today. “Police are continuing investigations, including completing the investigation papers,” he told Bernama when contacted today.

items.title: Hajiji: Sabah not up for debate, issue just a political tactic in Philippines
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 28 — Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the Sabah claim is often turned into a polemic by certain parties in the Philippines, particularly as a political tactic during election seasons. Hajiji stressed that Sabah’s status as part of the Malaysian Federation is final and has been recognised by the United Nations (UN), leaving no room for further dispute. But the people of Sabah have long decided to be part of Malaysia.

items.title: JPJ introduces new rules allowing rooftop, commercial spaces for driving training
items.summary: BALING, March 28 — The Road Transport Department Malaysia has introduced new guidelines for the establishment of Driving Institutes (IMs), including allowing training circuits to be built in high-rise buildings, warehouses and commercial premises. JPJ Director-General Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli said the guidelines were developed in line with the Motor Vehicles (Driving Schools) Rules 1992 and the Road Transport Act 1987, aimed at strengthening the regulation of the driving institute industry. Aedy Fadly said interested parties are welcome to submit applications at any time, including proposals to convert unused land, buildings or large warehouses into driving institutes.

items.title: PKR: Nurul Izzah resignation rumours just ‘fake news’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 28 — Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) has dismissed as “fake news” swirling rumours that its deputy president, Nurul Izzah Anwar, has resigned from the post. “Fake news,” Fahmi said simply. Nurul Izzah, who is the daughter of PKR president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, secured the deputy presidency in last year’s party elections.
